Prism Analytics

Democratizing real-time data intelligence for enterprise growth

STAFF DATA ENGINEER

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) · Full-time · $240,000 - $310,000 base + equity

Prism Analytics is seeking a Staff Data Engineer to lead our data platform architecture initiatives and

mentor a team of 5-7 engineers. This is a technical leadership role for an engineer with 8+ years of

experience who has designed and shipped large-scale data systems. You'll drive technical strategy

across multiple teams, establish best practices for our data lake and streaming infrastructure, and

directly influence product roadmap decisions. This role reports to the VP of Engineering and requires

both hands-on contributions and cross-functional leadership.

ABOUT US

Prism Analytics is a Series B data infrastructure company founded in 2019, helping enterprise clients

build modern data platforms that power real-time decision-making. We serve Fortune 500 companies
across financial services, retail, and healthcare with cloud-native analytics solutions. Our team of 80+

engineers focuses on reliability, scalability, and developer experience.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design and architect scalable data pipeline solutions serving 50+ petabytes of monthly data
ingestion, leading technical design reviews and RFC processes across engineering teams

Own the evolution of our streaming data platform (Kafka + Flink), establishing patterns and
frameworks that multiple teams adopt; mentor engineers on distributed systems trade-offs and

failure scenarios

Drive migrations and optimizations of legacy batch pipelines to modern architecture (dbt +

Airflow on Snowflake), reducing query costs by 30%+ and establishing governance standards

Lead incident response for critical data availability issues; post-incident, establish preventive
measures and update runbooks across teams

Establish technical standards for data modeling, documentation, and observability; conduct
quarterly audits of platform health across 200+ production pipelines

Mentor Staff/Senior engineers on complex problems; conduct architecture reviews and code
audits for high-risk features affecting data platform reliability

Collaborate with Product and Customer Success to define platform roadmap; translate
customer requirements into technical strategy and resource allocation

Champion data quality and observability culture; design and implement monitoring frameworks

using custom Python tooling and open-source solutions

REQUIRED

 8+ years of professional experience in data engineering with 3+ years at Staff, Principal, or

Staff-equivalent IC level leading cross-team initiatives

Proven expertise with Apache Spark and SQL; demonstrated ability to optimize complex

queries and design efficient data models at petabyte scale

Strong production experience with workflow orchestration (Airflow or equivalent) managing

100+ interdependent DAGs with SLA monitoring

Advanced Python programming skills; comfort writing maintainable code that other engineers

trust and reuse across teams

Hands-on experience with at least one cloud data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, or

Redshift); deep knowledge of optimization techniques, cost monitoring, and architectural trade-

offs

Demonstrated ability to influence technical decisions and mentor engineers; experience

establishing best practices and driving adoption across engineering teams

NICE TO HAVE

Experience designing and scaling Kafka or Pulsar-based streaming architectures in production;

understanding of exactly-once semantics, backpressure, and disaster recovery

Proficiency with dbt for analytical modeling and testing; experience building transformation
workflows for analytics and machine learning applications

Experience with Kubernetes or containerized data workloads; familiarity with monitoring and
logging in distributed systems (DataDog, Prometheus, ELK)

Published technical writing or public speaking about data engineering; contributions to open-
source data tools

TECH STACK

Apache Spark   Airflow   dbt

  Snowflake   Kafka   Python   SQL   Flink   DataDog   Kubernetes

BENEFITS

Competitive equity grant (0.25% - 0.50% for Staff level) vesting over 4 years with 1-year cliff

Comprehensive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% company coverage for
employees; HSA matching up to $2,000/year

401(k) matching: 4% of salary

Unlimited PTO + 10 company holidays; 6 weeks paid parental leave for all parents

Annual learning budget of $5,000 for conferences, certifications, or online courses

Home office setup stipend ($2,000 one-time for remote employees)

Monthly gym/wellness reimbursement ($50/month)

Free catered lunch 3 days/week + snacks; quarterly team offsites and quarterly engineering
gatherings

HOW TO APPLY

 Apply via our careers page at www.prismanalytics.com/careers or email engineering-

careers@prismanalytics.com with your resume, a brief cover letter describing your experience with

large-scale data systems, and a portfolio or GitHub link. We review applications on a rolling basis;

expected timeline is 4-5 weeks from application to offer decision.
